key_insight: A fully automated deep learning pipeline can precisely segment retinal atrophy and ellipsoid zone changes on OCT to quantify geographic atrophy biomarkers at pixel level.
clinical_relevance: This could improve real-world monitoring of GA in AMD by making structural biomarker measurement more consistent, repeatable, and scalable for trials and routine care.
method: Three semantic segmentation models detect RPE loss, EZ loss/interruptions, and Bruch's membrane
Trained on 298 diverse SD-OCT volumes spanning GA, intermediate AMD, neovascular AMD, and healthy eyes
Validated with an external dataset and additional repeatability, reader-reliability, and density-sensitivity analyses
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong performance, including Dice scores of 0.88 for RPE loss and 0.87 for EZ loss, Pearson's r > 0.99, sub-pixel EZ thickness deviation of 2.15 µm, and ICC > 0.98.
watch_item: Performance was strong in validation, but real-world deployment still depends on scanner/site consistency, image quality, and careful calibration across different OCT acquisition protocols.
paper_title: Fully Automated High-Precision Segmentation of Retinal Atrophy and Ellipsoid Zone Thickness in OCT: A Reliable Tool for Real-World GA Monitoring
